summary.headline: Ilona, your Galaxy-led carousel rotation is hitting saturation — two ads need replacing this week.

top_fatigued_ads.why_fatiguing: Galaxy hero shot recycled across multiple placements with near-identical 'Reimagined' headline pattern. The 'Shop Now' CTA repeats across the set and all roads lead to the same Galaxy PDP — Meta's algorithm is reading this as one creative, not five.
top_fatigued_ads.action_reason: Diminishing returns on a hero SKU that's been the centerpiece of the rotation. Rest it for 14 days, then relaunch with a styling-led angle, not a product-led one.

top_fatigued_ads.headline_or_hook: Sculptural tailoring for the modern woman
top_fatigued_ads.why_fatiguing: 'Sculptural tailoring' as a hook has appeared in three consecutive launches. The phrase is brand-true but Meta audiences scrolling past have seen the same flatlay-on-grey treatment too many times this quarter.
top_fatigued_ads.action_reason: The garment merits more screen time — but in motion. Swap the still flatlay for a 6-second runway clip with the same headline overlay.

top_fatigued_ads.why_fatiguing: 'New Season Arrivals' is the most generic line a luxury house can run — it doesn't earn the click against Toteme or Khaite using the same headline this week. The carousel format also mirrors ad-1.
top_fatigued_ads.action_reason: Replace the headline with a dated drop reference ('SS25 Drop 02. Live now.') to create urgency without discounting.

top_fatigued_ads.why_fatiguing: Editorial styling carries this further than the others, but the 'Made for moments' line is a category cliché — every occasionwear brand from Reformation up is using a variant of it.
top_fatigued_ads.action_reason: Keep the visual, sharpen the copy. Specify the moment — 'Made for the rehearsal dinner' converts harder than vague aspiration.

top_fatigued_ads.why_fatiguing: Process-led storytelling is your strongest performer in the set — it's the only ad here doing brand work, not just promotion. Watch it past day 30; until then, scale.
top_fatigued_ads.action_reason: This is the format Roland Mouret should be leaning into. Atelier footage + the Galaxy lineage = differentiated against every other tailoring DTC ad.

ad_to_scale.reason: Lowest fatigue score (45), strongest brand-story angle in the set. 'Behind the seam' has runway to push budget +40% before saturation hits, and it builds the kind of equity a flatlay never will.

ad_to_kill.reason: Galaxy hero is at 83 — critical. Same dress, same shot, same 'Reimagined' headline across the rotation. Pause it now, rest 14 days, relaunch with movement and a new angle.

replacement_concepts.hook: The dress that built a house.
replacement_concepts.angle: Heritage-led story positioning Galaxy as cultural artefact, not seasonal product. Pulls focus away from 'new' and toward 'iconic' — the territory only Roland Mouret can own.
replacement_concepts.primary_text: Twenty years. One silhouette. The Galaxy dress wasn't designed to trend — it was designed to outlast them. Cut from double-bonded crepe, sculpted to the body, worn by every woman who refuses to disappear in a room. This is the dress that built the house. Still made the same way. Still made for you.
replacement_concepts.visual_direction: Archive footage cross-cut with present-day atelier — same pattern piece, different decades. End on a single Galaxy in motion against deep charcoal.
replacement_concepts.fills_gap: Replaces the product-led Galaxy push (ad-1) with a heritage angle that can't be copied by younger DTC tailoring brands.

replacement_concepts.hook: Cut once. Worn for a decade.
replacement_concepts.angle: Anti-fast-fashion positioning that justifies the price point through longevity, not luxury signalling. Speaks to the cost-per-wear customer.
replacement_concepts.primary_text: We don't drop forty styles a month. We cut pieces you'll wear in 2034. Every Roland Mouret garment is pattern-graded by hand, finished in our London atelier, and built on a block that's been refined since 2005. Buy one. Wear it everywhere. That's the math.
replacement_concepts.visual_direction: Static hero shot of a single tailored jacket on a wooden hanger, slow zoom. Copy overlays in ivory serif. No model.
replacement_concepts.fills_gap: The current rotation has no value-justification creative — every ad sells aspiration but none answers 'why £1,200?'

replacement_concepts.hook: The black tie invitation just landed.
replacement_concepts.angle: Specific-occasion targeting that replaces 'Made for moments' (ad-4) with an actual moment. Search-intent copy disguised as editorial.
replacement_concepts.primary_text: Wedding season opens in six weeks. The brief: black tie, summer, photographs that will be looked at for thirty years. The answer: column silhouettes, structured shoulders, fabrics that don't crease in the car. Five styles, ready to ship in 48 hours. Find yours before the RSVP deadline.
replacement_concepts.visual_direction: Editorial carousel — five dresses, five settings (terrace, ballroom, garden, yacht, chapel). Shot in available light, not studio.
replacement_concepts.fills_gap: No occasion-specific creative in the live set despite Q2 wedding season being Roland Mouret's strongest commercial window.
